headline: Kuching Wetlands Ramsar Site Loses 340 Hectares to Encroachment
context: A 2025 SFC aerial survey found 340 hectares of the Kuching Wetlands National Park — a RAMSAR-designated site — cleared for aquaculture and settlement encroachment.

cards.big: Kuching Wetlands is one of Malaysia's 7 RAMSAR-designated sites.
cards.sub: 340 hectares — 5% of the total area — have been cleared despite international treaty protection.

cards.big: Sarawak Forest Department has 23 rangers for 6,610 hectares.
cards.sub: Patrol coverage is 2.1 visits per month per zone — insufficient to detect incremental clearing.

cards.big: Proboscis monkey population in the wetlands fell from 400 to 280 since 2018.
cards.sub: Habitat fragmentation from clearing has isolated 3 sub-populations, reducing genetic exchange.

cards.big: RAMSAR non-compliance could trigger international review.
cards.sub: Malaysia has never had a RAMSAR site de-listed. The reputational cost would affect all 7 sites.

cards.big: International protection that cannot stop 340 hectares of clearing is international decoration.
Treaty obligations require domestic enforcement. RAMSAR status is only as strong as the ranger budget behind it.
